notes: Section 411, , , , created a 10-member Joint Committee on Congressional Operations.Pub. L. 91–510, title IV, § 40184 Stat. 1187Oct. 26, 1970
Section 412, , , , enumerated duties of Joint Committee.Pub. L. 91–510, title IV, § 40284 Stat. 1187Oct. 26, 1970

Section 412a, based on H. Res. No. 988, § 206, Ninety-third Congress, , enacted into permanent law by , ch. III, § 101, , , related to continuing study of jurisdiction of House standing committees by House members of Joint Committee, periodic report to House Committee on Rules, and contents and purposes of such report.Oct. 8, 1974Dec. 27, 1974Pub. L. 93–554, title I88 Stat. 1777
Section 413, , , , related to powers of Joint Committee, including rulemaking, issuing subpenas, and administering oaths.Pub. L. 91–510, title IV, § 40384 Stat. 1188Oct. 26, 1970

Section 414, , , , authorized Joint Committee to appoint and manage professional staff members and to utilize Government services, personnel, consultants, and experts.Pub. L. 91–510, title IV, § 40484 Stat. 1188Oct. 26, 1970
Section 415, , , , related to records of Joint Committee.Pub. L. 91–510, title IV, § 40584 Stat. 1188Oct. 26, 1970
Section 416, , , , established Office of Placement and Office Management which was subject to supervision and control of Joint Committee.Pub. L. 91–510, title IV, § 40684 Stat. 1189Oct. 26, 1970
Section 417, , , , directed that expenses of Joint Committee be paid from contingent fund of House of Representatives.Pub. L. 91–510, title IV, § 40784 Stat. 1189Oct. 26, 1970
